claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements provider-specific isolation, boundary enforcement, and evidence validation through multiple architectural layers preventing cross-provider model leakage and project mutations during evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: execution_policy sets writes_business_code=false, agent_instruction='boundary_controlled', project_mutation='forbidden' with allowed_write_roots
evidence.why: Defines the harness boundary contract that evidence agents must obey regardless of provider.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot captures git file digests before/after agent runs; diffBoundarySnapshots detects any mutations outside allowed_write_roots and converts them to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers
evidence.why: Runtime boundary enforcement proves the harness catches violations regardless of what tools the provider has available.

evidence.summary: executionConfig clears Claude tool restriction flags (claude_tools='', claude_allowed_tools='', claude_disallowed_tools='') when provider=claude; normalizes agent_tool_profile to boundary/research/full based on allowFullAgentTools flag
evidence.why: Shows Claude dispatch deliberately avoids relying on CLI tool flags for safety, using prompt contract and boundary enforcement instead.

evidence.summary: When provider='codex', executionConfig clears inherited Claude models: if (!model && !ai.codex_model && /\bclaude\b/i.test(ai.model)) ai.model = ''
evidence.why: Prevents cross-provider model leakage where a Claude model string might be passed to Codex.

evidence.summary: Agent execution loop feeds previousResults into buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt, so cross-checker and verifier see all prior evidence
evidence.why: Proves evidence agents run sequentially with result chaining, not isolated parallel runs.

evidence.summary: inspectEvidenceAgreement converts conflicting agent recommendations into EVIDENCE_AGENT_CONFLICT blockers instead of averaging or majority-voting
evidence.why: Shows evidence agent conflicts are preserved as blockers, not silently resolved.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues blocks PRD readiness if evidence scope=project but no repo-relative path provided, or if all evidence is external/user/unknown for existing-project claims
evidence.why: Enforces evidence scope validation preventing external/user evidence from proving project facts.

evidence.summary: unresolvedAssumptions converts assumptions to blockers unless confirmed with status='confirmed' or confirmed=true
evidence.why: Prevents assumptions from being promoted to facts without explicit verification.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentProvider maps provider aliases (anthropic->claude, openai->codex, shell/local->custom)
evidence.why: Shows provider configuration normalization prevents ambiguous provider strings.

evidence.summary: inspectAgentAdapterContract blocks execution on claude dangerously-skip-permissions or codex danger-full-access sandbox modes
evidence.why: Proves unsafe configurations create explicit blockers before agent dispatch.

evidence.summary: Codex dispatch test asserts provider='codex', no Claude model inheritance, no forced read-only sandbox
evidence.why: Test evidence proves Codex provider isolation actually works in practice.

evidence.summary: Claude dispatch test verifies claude_tools='', claude_allowed_tools='', claude_disallowed_tools='', and relies on boundary enforcement instead of tool flags
evidence.why: Test confirms Claude dispatch deliberately avoids CLI tool restrictions.

evidence.summary: Claude full tool profile test validates that agent_tool_profile='full' requires allowFullAgentTools=true authorization
evidence.why: Proves full semantic mode requires explicit opt-in, not default behavior.

evidence.summary: boundary violation test spawns an agent that writes unexpected-project-write.txt; dispatch detects mutation, sets boundary.project_mutation='violated', and blocks readiness
evidence.why: Test proves boundary snapshot catches violations even when agent succeeds otherwise.

evidence.summary: git-backed boundary test initializes git repo, agent writes untracked-mutation.txt, boundary snapshot detects added file and creates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker
evidence.why: Proves git-backed file tracking catches untracked mutations.

evidence.summary: Contains 20+ dispatch artifacts (dispatch-20260531*, dispatch-20260601*) showing real stress-test runs with actual provider invocations
evidence.why: Artifact evidence proves demand evidence dispatch has been exercised in real scenarios, not just in theory.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states Claude provider dispatch does NOT pass --allowedTools/--disallowedTools as safety measures; agent_tool_profile only expresses semantic mode; real safety comes from prompt contract, artifact root, and boundary snapshots
evidence.why: Design documentation confirms the implementation intent matches the code.

evidence.summary: Evidence records must declare scope; project evidence requires repo-relative path; external evidence cannot prove existing-project facts
evidence.why: Documented evidence validation rules match the router implementation.

risks: If boundary snapshot has false positives or false negatives, violations could be missed or valid runs blocked.
If git-backed file tracking fails in non-git repos, mutations might go undetected.
If evidence agents cannot parse the prompt contract correctly, they might violate boundary despite instructions.
If provider-specific normalization has edge cases, model leakage could still occur in untested provider combinations.
missing: Stress-test logs or CI evidence showing boundary violations caught in production demand dispatch runs.
Evidence of custom provider adapter validation in real scenarios.
Documentation of how permission-mode interacts with evidence agent tool profile in practice.

result.notes: The implementation has solid architectural layers for provider isolation (config normalization, model clearing, boundary snapshots, evidence scope validation) backed by comprehensive tests covering provider-specific isolation, boundary violations, git-backed tracking, and evidence validation. Real dispatch artifacts exist showing multiple stress-test runs. Primary gap is production evidence of boundary violations being caught in CI or real demand workflows.
